_Toc35420380 \h 9 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Jeremy Rake 0 3 /1 7 /2020 0.0 Original document. Jeremy Rake 04/03/2020 0.1 Updates after functional review MItchell Bluford 6 / 0 8/2020 0. 2 Updates to system code naming structure Mitchell Bluford 6/17/2020 0.3 Added assumptions per development request Blake Coram 07/10/20 0.4 Updates based on development detail feedback regarding Pick Location Detail tracking. Mitchell Bluford 7/15/2020 0.5 Updates per development request regarding EX01 overlap Jeremy Rake 12/10/2020 2.0 CR updates to Pack iLPN from Active Mitchell Bluford 2/9/2021 2.1 Updating Pck iLPN tran parm EX 11 – Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment McLane divisions distribute cigarette items that are slow moving and therefore cannot hold full packs (cases) of these items in pre-stamped locations . Instead McLane replenishes unstamped active pick locations from case pick that can later be allocated at a unit (carton) level to replenish specific jurisdiction specific post stamp active pick locations. Therefore, McLane requires the ability to replenish cigarette inventory from one active location to another active location . WMOS is enhanced with a custom INT to allow inventory to be allocated from an unstamped pick location to a pre stamped jurisdiction specific location at a unit (carton) level. In addition WMOS customizes INT3 replenishment processing to ensure that when less than case ( pack ) quantity is needed WMOS only replenishes ful l pack quantity from case pick to jurisdiction active if the nee d exists (no rounding) and if the need is less WMOS first replenishes unstamped active before creating active to active replenishment. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y Inventory Need Types (INT s) EX11 introduces a custom Inventory Need Type (INT) for Active to Active Replenishment tasks. WMOS customizes wave replenishment generation by introducing an extended origin for active replenishment which allows inventory to be replenished from one active location pick determination zone (PDZ) to another active PDZ location. Leveraging base allocation priorities Active to Active replenishment INT is generated during the waving process and utilizes base task creation rules to create tasks to move inventory from one pick location to another. Assumptions Locations and process mentioned in this document are only for cigarettes management area. Each pre-stamped cigarette jurisdiction has dedicated active pick locations. In WMOS, this is determined by configuring pick determinization zone (PDZ) per jurisdiction and assigning PDZ to corresponding locations. Only locations in the unstamped active are considered for custom logic in EX11. Allocation Priority for custom INT- 99 is configured with proper unstamped PDZ as allocation source Standard allocation functions are supported for custom INT . Active to Active replenishment INT is only generated through the waving process . Base task creation rules are leveraged for custom INT task generation . Task action and standard task execution functions are supported for custom INT . Inventory moves from a jurisdiction specific location to another jurisdiction location must be manual process and is out of scope of this extension . EX11 custom replenishment process is mutuall y exclusive from EX05 replenishments . EX11 enablement is configured at a warehouse level and therefore is applicable to all waves. Allocation UOM for cigarette INT-3 will be Packs . Allocation UOM for custom Active to Active Replenishment will be Units. Unique cigarette SKUs will be assigned to one permanent active location with in the Unstamped Active PDZ. Soft allocation is out of scope for this extension. While custom EX11 item attribute substituted replenishments are pending for a pick location, the location is temporarily multi-SKU. During EX11 replenishment allocation, WM determines the required replenishment path of inventory. This may include generation of EX01 INT 3 replenishment. EX11 only generates the custom INT 99 replenishment allocation. The detail around execution and exceptions is handled as part of EX01. EX11 Pack iLPN from Active changes are only supported in RF Tasked mode. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX01 should w ork in conjunction with EX11 when allocating INT3 directly to jurisdiction specific active locations . EX05 and EX11 extensions will be used in same wave but against different items . Wave and allocation processing should support both. Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Distribution Order LineItem -> WaveAllocationType Allocation Type for Inventory Allocation P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Wave Allocation ￼ Process Steps WMOS wave allocation identifies opportunities for replenishment per base requirements WMOS checks if allocation type is flagged for custom less than case allocation else proceeds with base replenishment I F allocation type is flagged for less than case allocation WMOS checks if item demand can be met from unstamped active location and creates INT 99 Active to Active replenishment IF item demand cannot be met from unstamped active location, WMOS enters custom INT3 processing to over allocate to a full standard pack quantity to the unstamped active location I F there is not sufficient inventory for INT3 replenishment to meet item demand WMOS continues to allocate from Reserve using base INT9 Replenish Case Pick from reserve allocation IF inventory is insufficient to fulfill demand from reserve , WMOS invokes base shortage updates per base Process Steps UI Tasks UI – Cancel Active to Active Replenishment Task   See section 4.1 Tasks UI – Cancel Replenishment Task in the MCLN EX01 Cigarettes Inventory Conversion extension specification document. Custom Active to Active replenishment allocations/tasks generated through EX11 are expected to follow the EX01 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. Rebuild Wizard UI – Rebuild Case Pick or Active Location (To Be Filled) See section 4. 2 Rebuild Wizard UI – Rebuild Case Pick or Active Location (To Be Filled) in the MCLN EX01 Cigarettes Inventory Conversion extension specification document. Custom Active to Active replenishment allocations/tasks generated through EX11 are expected to follow the EX01 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Pack iLPN from Active Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI N Mobile (DM) N Enablement IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF RF Pack iLPN from Active transaction parameter 50 3 2 -50 3 2 = Y THEN the functionality detailed in section 6.1 is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 6.1 is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in section 6.1 is disabled Screen 1 - Location Confirmation Screen EX 11 suppresses base Location C onfirmation screen . Display N/A Ctrl Keys N/A Validations N/A Updates Per base. Navigation WMOS proceeds to base iLPN Prompt screen. Screen 2 – iLPN Prompt Add custom validation for iLPN scan. Display N/A Ctrl Keys N/A Validations WMOS validates that the scanned iLPN does not contain a quantity greater than or equal to C-E11-011 IF LPN_DETAIL.SIZE_VALUE > = C -E11-011 byte 1-3 (30) THEN WMOS displays CUST/1101 “Qty is greater than 30 Cartons” ELSE WMOS accepts iLPN scan Updates Per base. Navigation WMOS proceeds to Quantity Prompt Screen. Screen 2 3 RF Fill Pick Location – Inventory Conversion Active to Active Replenishment End iLPN (Ctrl-E) Invok ed : WMOS re turns to base iLPN prompt screen to capture new container . Outputs Activity Tracking N/A PIX N/A LM Hooks N/A See section 6.1 RF Fill Pick Location – Inventory Conversion in the MCLN EX01 Cigarettes Inventory Conversion extension specification document. Custom Active to Active replenishment allocations /tasks generated through EX11 are expected to follow the EX01 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. RF Pack iLPN From Actv – Short Active to Active Replenishment During EX11 Active to Active replenishment execution, the inventory being pulled from case pick leverages RF Pack iLPN From Actv to execute the pull. In that process, as exception, the user may short the replenishment pull. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS shortage updates, WMOS must also revert any custom EX11 cigarette item replenishment updates performed. Note: as part of the replenishment generation which created the original allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists. When the task or allocation is completed, cancelled or shorted, the updates occur to the original and permanent Pick Location Detail with defined Item Attribute 1 value. The dummy PLD is decremented and ultimately deleted in that process. Enablement The modification is enabled if all below validation succeeds: IF         C-000-000 byte 1 is enabled (Global Extension Enablement) IF         C-000-EX11 byte 1 is enabled (Custom Specific Enablement) IF         C-000-EX01 byte 1 is enabled (Custom Specific Enablement) IF Parameter byte 502-502 for current RF Pack iLPN From Actv transaction is enabled IF ITEM_CBO.PROD_TYPE = C-EX1 (byte 1-3) (Cigarette item) THEN the functionality detailed in this section is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled Validations First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX01 custom cigarette item replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base shortage processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation.      Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being shorted is for an EX01 custom cigarette item replenishment need. If the replenishment being shorted is not for custom cigarette item replenishment, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base short replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below.    Updates As part of completing base shortage updates, WMOS performs the base updates to cancelation of the allocation or task. If the item being shorted is the same as the pick location being replenished, base location updates are also performed. Additionally, the decrementing of the To Be Filled quantity in the pick location is enhanced to decrement the item and quantity based on the custom cigarette item replenishment process . For instance , when the allocation is created for the Item Attribute 1 as NULL, WMOS custom creates a dummy Pick Location Detail for that same combination of item and item attribute 1 as NULL which is not the permanently assigned item attribute of the location. During the short, the updates reflect the shorting and decrementing of the assigned Item Attribute 1 To Be Filled as well as the dummy PLD To Be Filled. If the shorting of this process results in no further To Be Filled on the dummy PLD of item attribute 1 as NULL, then this PLD is also removed. Outputs Activity Tracking N/A PIX N/A LM Hooks N/A RF Fill Pick Location – Short Active to Active Replenishment See section 6.3 RF Fill Pick Location – Short in the MCLN EX01 Cigarettes Inventory Conversion extension specification document. Custom Active to Active replenishment allocations/tasks generated through EX11 are expected to follow the EX01 inventory conversion execution and exception processes. Process Change/Addition Custom Active to Active Replenishment Inventory Need Type Generation (INT) A custom Active to Active Replenishment Inve ntory Need Type (INT) is created during allocation for cigarett e inventory . During allocation for slow moving cigarette items there may not be sufficient inventory to fulfill order needs from the active pick location for a given jurisdiction. WMOS extends active replenishment origin for pre-stamped locations to allow replenishments from unstamped active locations. Allocation priority and parameters for this INT are set leveraging the base allocation priorities, rules, and parameters. Additionally, WMOS may not have sufficient inventory in the unstamped active location to replenish pre-stamped active pick location and will generate cascaded replenishments. In some cases, to satisfy item demand a replenishment may be needed from the case pick location. WMOS is customized to process Case Pick to Active INT3 r eplenishments based whether the item need is equal to that of a full standard pack quantity . When less than a standard pack quantity WMOS is customized to over allocate a full case to the unstamped active location . Furthermore, if the need requires a replenishment from reserve to generate associated INT3 or INT 99 replenishments WMOS will allocate INT9 per base allocation priorities, rules, and parameters. Note: as part of the replenishment generation in this section which creates the allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists. When the task or allocation is completed, cancelled or shorted, the updates occur to the original and permanent Pick Location Detail with defined Item Attribute 1 value. The dummy PLD is decremented and ultimately deleted in that process.    Active to Active Replenishment INT Generation – Wave Process Enablement IF C-00 0 -000 byte 1 is enabled (global extension enablement) THEN IF C-00 0 - EX11 byte 1 is enabled (extension specific enablement) AND B- 325 EX11 byte 1 is enabled ( allocation type enablement) THEN the functionality detailed in this section 7.1 is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in the section 7.1 is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled Active to Active Replenishment – INT - 99 Allocation Generation During waving, WMOS checks for allocation type flagged for less than case allocation processing using B- 325 Byte 1 - 1 . This allocation type is configured leveraging base allocati ons for INT- 99 Active to Active replenishment. Using this allocation type WMOS determines if there is a replenishment opportunity to meet item demand by allocating inventory from unstamped active location to jurisdiction specific location. WMOS first determines if item demand can be met from the Unstamped Active pick determination zone leveraging the base allocation priority set up for INT- 99 . If there is sufficient inventory to meet item demand WMOS generates custom INT- 99 Active to Active Replenishment allocation to replenish units from unstamped active location to the replenishment origin jurisdiction active location . The Allocation Priorities UI is customized to allow INT- 99 Active to Active Replenishment allocations. For INT- 99 McLane allocates less than case quantities only meaning Allocation UOM is set to “Units” for allocating inventory from the unstamped pick location to the jurisdiction pick location. Unstamped pick locations are the only location less than standard pack quantity cigarettes are allocated from and therefore WMOS is configured to not exceed the need . Additionally, WMOS is customized to cascade replenishments for INT- 99 . Meaning if replenishment for jurisdiction active cannot be fulfilled from unstamped active location WMOS will attempt to replenish from the case pick and reserve location s per allocation priorities and parameters set for less than case allocation type. Therefore, d uring replenishment allocation, WMOS is customized to override active replenishment origin when allocating replenishments from case pick to active pick location (INT3). T hat is , when an INT 99 replenishment requires inventory to first be replenished from case pick WMOS will allocate INT3 replenishment to fulfill the demand . INT-3 processing for this allocation type is customized to replenish similar to post-stamp jurisdictions. WMOS only consider s unstamped active locations where B-331 (Pick Location Determination Zones) Code ID (PDZ) Byte 99 - 99 (EX11) = ‘Y’ as destination for custom INT-3 allocation and ignores the pre-stamped active origin . WMOS only process es INT3 with custom logic if the demand quantity is less than a standard pack quantity and requires over allocation to the unstamped active location. Finally, WMOS should consider replenishment opportunities from reserve to case pick (INT-9) in order to satisfy item demand needed by subsequent case pick and active locations. Note while INT-9 are supported as part of EX11 they are processed per base allocation priorities, parameters, and rules and no customization is done to INT-9 processing as part of this modification. Custom INT-3 is processe d differently for INT- 99 depending on whether the need is for a full pack or not: I F demand for EX11 item is for a full pack ( QTY Demand = Packs ITEM_PACKAGE.QUANTITY) THEN INT-3 is generated directly to jurisdiction active location per base INT 3 processing for unstamped active and as per EX01 custom INT 3 processing for pre-stamped jurisdiction active I F demand for EX11 item is less than pack quantity ( QTY Demand < Packs ITEM_PACKAGE.QUANTITY) THEN WMOS invoke s custom INT-3 processing to allocate a full pack quantity from case pick to unstamped active thus over allocating up to the full pack quantity and not directly to jurisdiction active origin . WM subsequently generates custom INT 99 for the exact demand quantity which is less than pack from unstamped active to jurisdiction specific active. IF demand for EX11 item is for greater than full pack ( QTY Demand > Packs ITEM_PACKAGE.QUANTITY) THEN WMOS follows custom INT-3 base processing for unstamped active and as per EX01 custom INT 3 processing for pre-stamped jurisdiction active for the full standard pack to jurisdiction active location and allocate s the remaining less than standard pack quantity from case pick to unstamped active Example Custom Allocation: Below is an example of the expected allocation output from a wave using custom INT- 99 generation and INT3 custom processing . Demand for ITEM_1 is 10 units (cartons) , Allocation Type Less Than Case No existing inventory in unstamped active or jurisdiction active for ITEM_1 Sequencing of custom allocation follows the configured allocation parameters and priorities as relevant to the wave replenishment template/order/item hierarchy in base. Allocation is created for INT3 and processed with custom INT3 logic to replenish the unstamped active location ( per B-331 Byte 99 - 99 = ‘Y’) over allocating to a standard pack quantity (full case) Allocation is created for custom INT- 99 to replenish jurisdiction active from unstamped active for 10 units Location inventory updates are processed per base Note: in order to achieve the above process, when the allocation is created for the Item Attribute 1 as NULL, WMOS custom creates a dummy Pick Location Detail for that same combination of item and item attribute 1 as NULL which is not the permanently assigned item attribute of the location. This may require net new creation of a PLD which does not previously exist, or updates to an existing PLD for that NULL attribute.  Wave Outputs INT 99 Mapping: Field Data Source ALLOC_INVN_DTL_ID undone. Note: as part of the replenishment generation in this section which creates the allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists. When the task or allocation is completed, cancelled or shorted, the updates occur to the original and permanent Pick Location Detail with defined Item Attribute 1 value. The dummy PLD is decremented and ultimately deleted in that process. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Cigarette Replenishment : Custom INT3 Replenishment to over allocate to Unstamped Active and INT 99 to replenish Jurisdiction Acti ve – Extension Enabled WMOS generates INT3 and uses custom INT3 processing to over allocate to a full standard pack to unstamped active location. WMOS also generates INT 99 replenishment from unstamped active location not exceeding need to Jurisdiction active location. Cigarette Replenishment: INT 3 standard pack qty to Jurisdiction Active WMOS generates INT3 and uses base INT3 allocation to allocate full standard pack quantity directly to Jurisdiction Active location. Cigarette Replenishment INT3 & INT 99 : Full Pack INT3 & Overa llocated INT3 for INT 99 replenishment WMOS generates INT3 per base for full pack replenished to jurisdiction active. WMOS additionally generates INT3 allocation for custom processing to allocate remaining less than pack quantity from case pick to unstamped active. INT 99 generated for less tha n pack quantity allocation. Cigarette Reserve/Case Pick/Active Replenishment: INT9, Custom INT3, INT 99 for to replenish Jurisdiction Active WMOS generates INT 9 replenishment per base. WMOS custom INT3 processing is done to over allocate to standard pack quantity to unstamped active location . Additionally, WMOS generates custom INT 99 to replenish Jurisdiction Active location from Unstamped Active. Exceptions Condition Name Description and expectations Cigarette Active to Active INT 99 Replenishment Generation – Global Extension Flag Disable d WMOS proceeds with base replenishment allocation . No INT 99 generated. Cigarette Active to Active INT 99 Replenishment – Ex tension Flag Disable d WMOS proceeds with base replenishment allocation. No INT 99 generated. Cigarette Custom INT3 generation & Active to Active INT 99 Replenishment Generation – Global Extension Flag Disabled WMOS generates INT3 per base replenishment allocation. INT3 only generated to jurisdiction specific active. Cigarette Custom INT3 generation & Active to Active INT 99 Replenishment Generation – Extension Flag Disabled WMOS generates INT3 per base replenishment allocation. refore, McLane requires the ability to replenish cigarette inventory from one active location to another active location . WMOS is enhanced with a custom INT to allow inventory to be allocated from an unstamped pick location to a pre stamped jurisdiction specific location at a unit (carton) level.

In addition WMOS customizes INT3 replenishment processing to ensure that when less than case ( pack ) quantity is needed WMOS only replenishes ful l pack quantity from case pick to jurisdiction active if the nee d exists (no rounding) and if the need is less WMOS first replenishes unstamped active before creating active to active replenishment. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y Inventory Need Types (INT s) EX11 introduces a custom Inventory Need Type (INT) for Active to Active Replenishment tasks. WMOS customizes wave replenishment generation by introducing an extended origin for active replenishment which allows inventory to be replenished from one active location pick determination zone (PDZ) to another active PDZ location. Leveraging base allocation priorities Active to Active replenishment INT is generated during the waving process and utilizes base task creation rules to create tasks to move inventory from one pick location to another. Assumptions Locations and process mentioned in this document are only for cigarettes management area. Each pre-stamped cigarette jurisdiction has dedicated active pick locations. In WMOS, this is determined by configuring pick determinization zone (PDZ) per jurisdiction and assigning PDZ to corresponding locations. Only locations in the unstamped active are considered for custom logic in EX11. Allocation Priority for custom INT- 99 is configured with proper unstamped PDZ as allocation source Standard allocation functions are supported for custom INT . Active to Active replenishment INT is only generated through the waving process . Base task creation rules are leveraged for custom INT task generation . Task action and standard task execution functions are supported for custom INT . Inventory moves from a jurisdiction specific location to another jurisdiction location must be manual process and is out of scope of this extension . EX11 custom replenishment process is mutuall y exclusive from EX05 replenishments . EX11 enablement is configured at a warehouse level and therefore is applicable to all waves. Allocation UOM for cigarette INT-3 will be Packs . Allocation UOM for custom Active to Active Replenishment will be Units. Unique cigarette SKUs will be assigned to one permanent active location with in the Unstamped Active PDZ.

[Document: MCLN EX11 Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment_2.1]

igured at a warehouse level and therefore is applicable to all waves. Allocation UOM for cigarette INT-3 will be Packs . Allocation UOM for custom Active to Active Replenishment will be Units. Unique cigarette SKUs will be assigned to one permanent active location with in the Unstamped Active PDZ.

Soft allocation is out of scope for this extension. While custom EX11 item attribute substituted replenishments are pending for a pick location, the location is temporarily multi-SKU. During EX11 replenishment allocation, WM determines the required replenishment path of inventory. This may include generation of EX01 INT 3 replenishment. EX11 only generates the custom INT 99 replenishment allocation. The detail around execution and exceptions is handled as part of EX01. EX11 Pack iLPN from Active changes are only supported in RF Tasked mode. Dependencies Code Dependencies EX01 should w ork in conjunction with EX11 when allocating INT3 directly to jurisdiction specific active locations . EX05 and EX11 extensions will be used in same wave but against different items . Wave and allocation processing should support both. RF Pack iLPN From Actv – Short Active to Active Replenishment During EX11 Active to Active replenishment execution, the inventory being pulled from case pick leverages RF Pack iLPN From Actv to execute the pull. In that process, as exception, the user may short the replenishment pull. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS shortage updates, WMOS must also revert any custom EX11 cigarette item replenishment updates performed.

[Document: MCLN EX11 Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment_2.1]

g pulled from case pick leverages RF Pack iLPN From Actv to execute the pull. In that process, as exception, the user may short the replenishment pull. In that scenario, in addition to base WMOS shortage updates, WMOS must also revert any custom EX11 cigarette item replenishment updates performed.

Note: as part of the replenishment generation which created the original allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists. When the task or allocation is completed, cancelled or shorted, the updates occur to the original and permanent Pick Location Detail with defined Item Attribute 1 value. The dummy PLD is decremented and ultimately deleted in that process. Enablement The modification is enabled if all below validation succeeds: IF         C-000-000 byte 1 is enabled (Global Extension Enablement) IF         C-000-EX11 byte 1 is enabled (Custom Specific Enablement) IF         C-000-EX01 byte 1 is enabled (Custom Specific Enablement) IF Parameter byte 502-502 for current RF Pack iLPN From Actv transaction is enabled IF ITEM_CBO.PROD_TYPE = C-EX1 (byte 1-3) (Cigarette item) THEN the functionality detailed in this section is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled Validations First, WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX01 custom cigarette item replenishment logic. If any of the enablement features are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base shortage processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation.      Next, WMOS validates the replenishment being shorted is for an EX01 custom cigarette item replenishment need. If the replenishment being shorted is not for custom cigarette item replenishment, then WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base short replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the custom item substitution processing below.    Updates As part of completing base shortage updates, WMOS performs the base updates to cancelation of the allocation or task. If the item being shorted is the same as the pick location being replenished, base location updates are also performed. Additionally, the decrementing of the To Be Filled quantity in the pick location is enhanced to decrement the item and quantity based on the custom cigarette item replenishment process . For instance , when the allocation is created for the Item Attribute 1 as NULL, WMOS custom creates a dummy Pick Location Detail for that same combination of item and item attribute 1 as NULL which is not the permanently assigned item attribute of the location.

[Document: MCLN EX11 Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment_2.1]

garette item replenishment process . For instance , when the allocation is created for the Item Attribute 1 as NULL, WMOS custom creates a dummy Pick Location Detail for that same combination of item and item attribute 1 as NULL which is not the permanently assigned item attribute of the location.

During the short, the updates reflect the shorting and decrementing of the assigned Item Attribute 1 To Be Filled as well as the dummy PLD To Be Filled. If the shorting of this process results in no further To Be Filled on the dummy PLD of item attribute 1 as NULL, then this PLD is also removed. Process Change/Addition Custom Active to Active Replenishment Inventory Need Type Generation (INT) A custom Active to Active Replenishment Inve ntory Need Type (INT) is created during allocation for cigarett e inventory . During allocation for slow moving cigarette items there may not be sufficient inventory to fulfill order needs from the active pick location for a given jurisdiction. WMOS extends active replenishment origin for pre-stamped locations to allow replenishments from unstamped active locations. Allocation priority and parameters for this INT are set leveraging the base allocation priorities, rules, and parameters. Additionally, WMOS may not have sufficient inventory in the unstamped active location to replenish pre-stamped active pick location and will generate cascaded replenishments. In some cases, to satisfy item demand a replenishment may be needed from the case pick location. WMOS is customized to process Case Pick to Active INT3 r eplenishments based whether the item need is equal to that of a full standard pack quantity . When less than a standard pack quantity WMOS is customized to over allocate a full case to the unstamped active location . Furthermore, if the need requires a replenishment from reserve to generate associated INT3 or INT 99 replenishments WMOS will allocate INT9 per base allocation priorities, rules, and parameters. Note: as part of the replenishment generation in this section which creates the allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists.

nt generation in this section which creates the allocation, WMOS creates a custom dummy Pick Location Detail for the item with a NULL item attribute 1. This is to allow for appropriate allocation and task references. This custom created dummy PLD is only transient while the allocation/task exists.

The dummy PLD is decremented and ultimately deleted in that process.    Active to Active Replenishment INT Generation – Wave Process Enablement IF C-00 0 -000 byte 1 is enabled (global extension enablement) THEN IF C-00 0 - EX11 byte 1 is enabled (extension specific enablement) AND B- 325 EX11 byte 1 is enabled ( allocation type enablement) THEN the functionality detailed in this section 7.1 is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in the section 7.1 is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled Active to Active Replenishment – INT - 99 Allocation Generation During waving, WMOS checks for allocation type flagged for less than case allocation processing using B- 325 Byte 1 - 1 . This allocation type is configured leveraging base allocati ons for INT- 99 Active to Active replenishment. Using this allocation type WMOS determines if there is a replenishment opportunity to meet item demand by allocating inventory from unstamped active location to jurisdiction specific location. WMOS first determines if item demand can be met from the Unstamped Active pick determination zone leveraging the base allocation priority set up for INT- 99 . If there is sufficient inventory to meet item demand WMOS generates custom INT- 99 Active to Active Replenishment allocation to replenish units from unstamped active location to the replenishment origin jurisdiction active location . The Allocation Priorities UI is customized to allow INT- 99 Active to Active Replenishment allocations. For INT- 99 McLane allocates less than case quantities only meaning Allocation UOM is set to “Units” for allocating inventory from the unstamped pick location to the jurisdiction pick location. Unstamped pick locations are the only location less than standard pack quantity cigarettes are allocated from and therefore WMOS is configured to not exceed the need . Additionally, WMOS is customized to cascade replenishments for INT- 99 . Meaning if replenishment for jurisdiction active cannot be fulfilled from unstamped active location WMOS will attempt to replenish from the case pick and reserve location s per allocation priorities and parameters set for less than case allocation type.

[Document: MCLN EX11 Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment_2.1]

ized to cascade replenishments for INT- 99 . Meaning if replenishment for jurisdiction active cannot be fulfilled from unstamped active location WMOS will attempt to replenish from the case pick and reserve location s per allocation priorities and parameters set for less than case allocation type.

Therefore, d uring replenishment allocation, WMOS is customized to override active replenishment origin when allocating replenishments from case pick to active pick location (INT3). T hat is , when an INT 99 replenishment requires inventory to first be replenished from case pick WMOS will allocate INT3 replenishment to fulfill the demand . INT-3 processing for this allocation type is customized to replenish similar to post-stamp jurisdictions. WMOS only consider s unstamped active locations where B-331 (Pick Location Determination Zones) Code ID (PDZ) Byte 99 - 99 (EX11) = ‘Y’ as destination for custom INT-3 allocation and ignores the pre-stamped active origin . WMOS only process es INT3 with custom logic if the demand quantity is less than a standard pack quantity and requires over allocation to the unstamped active location. Finally, WMOS should consider replenishment opportunities from reserve to case pick (INT-9) in order to satisfy item demand needed by subsequent case pick and active locations. Note while INT-9 are supported as part of EX11 they are processed per base allocation priorities, parameters, and rules and no customization is done to INT-9 processing as part of this modification. Custom INT-3 is processe d differently for INT- 99 depending on whether the need is for a full pack or not: I F demand for EX11 item is for a full pack ( QTY Demand = Packs ITEM_PACKAGE.QUANTITY) THEN INT-3 is generated directly to jurisdiction active location per base INT 3 processing for unstamped active and as per EX01 custom INT 3 processing for pre-stamped jurisdiction active I F demand for EX11 item is less than pack quantity ( QTY Demand < Packs ITEM_PACKAGE.QUANTITY) THEN WMOS invoke s custom INT-3 processing to allocate a full pack quantity from case pick to unstamped active thus over allocating up to the full pack quantity and not directly to jurisdiction active origin . WM subsequently generates custom INT 99 for the exact demand quantity which is less than pack from unstamped active to jurisdiction specific active.

[Document: MCLN EX11 Cigarette Active to Active Replenishment_2.1]

l pack quantity from case pick to unstamped active thus over allocating up to the full pack quantity and not directly to jurisdiction active origin . WM subsequently generates custom INT 99 for the exact demand quantity which is less than pack from unstamped active to jurisdiction specific active.

IF demand for EX11 item is for greater than full pack ( QTY Demand > Packs ITEM_PACKAGE.QUANTITY) THEN WMOS follows custom INT-3 base processing for unstamped active and as per EX01 custom INT 3 processing for pre-stamped jurisdiction active for the full standard pack to jurisdiction active location and allocate s the remaining less than standard pack quantity from case pick to unstamped active Example Custom Allocation: Below is an example of the expected allocation output from a wave using custom INT- 99 generation and INT3 custom processing . Demand for ITEM_1 is 10 units (cartons) , Allocation Type Less Than Case No existing inventory in unstamped active or jurisdiction active for ITEM_1 Sequencing of custom allocation follows the configured allocation parameters and priorities as relevant to the wave replenishment template/order/item hierarchy in base. Allocation is created for INT3 and processed with custom INT3 logic to replenish the unstamped active location ( per B-331 Byte 99 - 99 = ‘Y’) over allocating to a standard pack quantity (full case) Allocation is created for custom INT- 99 to replenish jurisdiction active from unstamped active for 10 units Location inventory updates are processed per base Note: in order to achieve the above process, when the allocation is created for the Item Attribute 1 as NULL, WMOS custom creates a dummy Pick Location Detail for that same combination of item and item attribute 1 as NULL which is not the permanently assigned item attribute of the location.
